channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Germans mourn attack on Christmas market with no answers about why
item.description: MAGDEBURG, Germany (AP) — Germans on Saturday mourned a violent attack and their shaken sense of security after a Saudi doctor intentionally drove a black BMW into a Christmas market teeming with holiday shoppers, killing at least two people, including a small child, and injuring at least 60 others.
item.dc:creator: KIRSTEN GRIESHABER, SARA ABOUBAKR and VANESSA GERA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Government funding difficulties create gloom for federal workers before Christmas
item.description: ANNAPOLIS, Md. (AP) — Johnny Zuagar says he’s tried to hide his worries about a potential government shutdown from his three boys as he weighs how much to spend on Christmas presents. “I’ve got to keep a poker face,” Zuagar, a statistician at the U.S.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Santa and Mrs. Claus use military transports to bring Christmas to an Alaska Native village
item.description: YAKUTAT, Alaska (AP) — Forget the open-air sleigh overloaded with gifts and powered by flying reindeer. Santa and Mrs.
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Canyon de Chelly in Arizona will become latest national park unit to ban commercial air tours
item.description: Commercial air tours will soon be prohibited over Canyon de Chelly National Monument in northeastern Arizona under a plan approved this week by the Federal Aviation Administration and the National Park Service.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A judge says Missouri's abortion ban isn’t enforceable, but there's no start date for abortions
item.description: JEFFERSON CITY, Mo. (AP) — A judge ruled Friday that Missouri’s near-total abortion ban is unenforceable under a new constitutional amendment, though Planned Parenthood said the decision is still not sufficient for it to resume providing abortions in the state.
item.dc:creator: DAVID A. LIEB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US residents flock to Mexico bearing gifts for family members, but leery of security
item.description: That annual return has been tarnished in recent years by concerns about safety on Mexican roads, as drug cartels sow terror on towns along highways in central states like Guanajuato and Zacatecas, and in Michoacan and Guerrero to the west.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, Fox 5

item.title: 1 dead in small plane crash near home of NFL player Dion Dawkins
item.description: One person died in a small plane crash Thursday morning close to the house of Buffalo Bills offensive lineman Dion Dawkins, police said.
item.dc:creator: Aidan Joly, KRON4

item.title: California political operative allegedly acted as illegal agent of China: DOJ
item.description: A California man who helped an individual get elected to a local California city council is accused of acting as an illegal agent for the People's Republic of China.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House rejects Johnson's plan B to prevent shutdown
item.description: The House on Thursday torpedoed Speaker Mike Johnson’s (R-La.) plan B to avert a government shutdown and suspend the debt limit, dealing a blow to the GOP leader and catapulting the conference back to square one as it stares down Friday's funding deadline.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Shutdown odds rapidly rise as House seeks plan B
item.description: The odds of a shutdown were rapidly growing on Thursday after President-elect Trump upended the talks by urging GOP lawmakers to shutter the government unless Democrats agree to a clean stopgap spending bill that raises or abolishes the debt limit.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Snow, ice and strong winds make for dangerous conditions in upper Midwest
item.description: BISMARCK, N.D. (AP) — A fast-moving winter storm brought snow, ice, strong winds and bitter cold to much of the upper Midwest, snarling traffic in the Twin Cities and forcing North Dakota officials to shut down an interstate highway.
item.dc:creator: JACK DURA and JIM SALTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: In Florida, a race is on to save the Everglades and protect a key source of drinking water
item.description: EVERGLADES, Fla. (AP) — In a region of Florida known as the River of Grass, John Kominoski plops into hip-deep waters. Blobs of brown periphyton – a mishmash of algae, bacteria and other organisms – carpet the surface.
item.dc:creator: DORANY PINEDA and REBECCA BLACKWELL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New legislation will provide Social Security benefits to more retirees
item.description: The U.S. Senate is expected to pass legislation to expand Social Security benefits to nearly three million retirees before lawmakers leave for the holiday break.
item.dc:creator: Jessi Turnure, KTLA 5

item.title: 76ers win city council nod to build $1.3B downtown stadium and leave South Philadelphia
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — The Philadelphia 76ers won city council approval on Thursday for a $1.3 billion downtown arena despite vocal opposition from nearby Chinatown residents and other activists. Council voted 12-5 to approve the project at a packed meeting at City Hall.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Kansas restaurant worker caught chopping meat on ground, owner explains why
item.description: The video shows an employee cutting up or hammering meat on the sidewalk or drive-thru pavement area right outside Tryyaki.
item.dc:creator: Dave D'Marko, ABC 4

item.title: Los Angeles deputy mayor allegedly made bomb threat to City Hall
item.description: Federal agents searched the home of Los Angeles Deputy Mayor Brian Williams on Tuesday following allegations that he made a bomb threat to City Hall earlier this year.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, ABC 4

item.title: Amazon workers strike at facilities around the country as Teamsters seek contract
item.description: Amazon says it doesn’t expect an impact on its operations during what the union calls the largest strike against the company in U.S. history.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, Fox 5

item.title: First trailer for James Gunn's 'Superman' teases DC characters rarely seen in live-action movies
item.description: The trailer — which debuted one day after another, much shorter teaser — was posted to the film's social media pages and the DC Comics YouTube page at 9 a.m. Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, Fox 5

item.title: Major discount retailer begins 'going out of business' sales across US
item.description: After filing for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection and closing hundreds of stores, Big Lots is preparing to close all of its locations, the company confirmed on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KTLA 5

item.title: Oklahoma executes man convicted of grisly murder of 10-year-old girl
item.description: The 25th and final execution of the year took place in Oklahoma Thursday morning when Kevin Ray Underwood was put to death by lethal injection for murdering a 10-year-old girl.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Year's Eve ball drop numbers unveiled in NYC
item.description: Even though New Year’s Eve is nearly two weeks away, there was an official celebration in the location best known worldwide for ringing in the new year.
item.dc:creator: James Ford, KRON4

item.title: Suspect in killing of UnitedHealthcare CEO will return to New York to face murder charges
item.description: The suspect in the killing of UnitedHealthcare’s CEO will return to New York to face murder charges after agreeing to be extradited Thursday during a court appearance in Pennsylvania where he was arrested last week after five days on the run.
item.dc:creator: Mark Scolforo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Fani Willis disqualified as prosecutor on Trump Georgia election case
item.description: A Georgia appeals court booted Fulton County District Attorney Fani Willis's (D) office from the 2020 election interference case against President-elect Trump on Thursday due to her relationship with a top prosecutor on the case.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: France rape trial: Gisèle Pelicot speaks out after ex-husband, 50 others found guilty
item.description: Gisèle Pelicot spoke of her “very difficult ordeal” after 51 men were all found guilty Thursday in the drugging-and-rape trial that turned her into a feminist hero, expressing support for other victims of sexual violence whose cases don't get such attention and “whose stories remain untold.
item.dc:creator: John Leicester, Fox 5

item.title: Madison, Wisconsin, school shooting suspect was in contact with man accused of plotting his own attack: report
item.description: The teen shooter at a private Christian school in Madison, Wisconsin, was in contact with a man in California whom authorities say was plotting a mass shooting with her, a report says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: FAA announces temporary restrictions on drone flights in New Jersey following influx of sightings
item.description: The FAA issued a notice on Wednesday temporarily restricting drone flights over parts of New Jersey until mid-January.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The egg industry kills 350 million male chicks a year. New tech offers an alternative
item.description: Every year the U.S. egg industry kills about 350 million male chicks because, while the fuzzy little animals are incredibly cute, they will never lay eggs, so have little monetary value.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO murder: Death penalty on the table for suspect Luigi Mangione who faces federal charges
item.description: United Healthcare CEO murder suspect Luigi Mangione appeared in court Thursday and waived extradition, paving way to return to New York.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US effort to curb China's and Russia's access to advanced computer chips 'inadequate,' report finds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Commerce Department's efforts to curb China's and Russia's access to American-made advanced computer chips have been “inadequate” and will need more funding to stymie their ability to manufacture advanced weapons, according to a report published Wednesday by the Senate's Perman
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Former top aide to New York City's mayor expected in court on criminal charges, AP sources say
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A former top aide to New York City Mayor Eric Adams is expected in court Thursday to face corruption charges in the latest blow to an administration beset by searches, resignations and the mayor's own indictment.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE and JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What is 'coffee badging?' Workplace trend seen as protest to return-to-office push
item.description: As the post-pandemic return-to-office push gathers steam, again, workers are quietly mounting a pushback of their own.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Mexico tightens bonds with immigrant community in US
item.description: The government of Mexico is strengthening bonds with its citizens in the United States, pledging to assist them at a time immigrant communities could be coming under increased scrutiny here.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, Fox 5

item.title: Louisiana patient has first severe bird flu case in US: health officials
item.description: Human cases of bird flu have been reported in the U.S. before, but infections were mild in those patients.
item.dc:creator: Trinity Velazquez, Fox 5

item.title: First severe bird flu case confirmed in US: CDC
item.description: Human cases of bird flu have been reported in the U.S. before, but infections were mild in those patients.
item.dc:creator: Trinity Velazquez, WGN 9

item.title: This Colorado restaurant only has 2 items on the menu
item.description: A Michelin-starred chef co-opened a restaurant in Denver with only two items on the menu: a burger and fries.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Rhodes, Fox 5

item.title: Key details about the man accused of killing UnitedHealthcare's CEO
item.description: Luigi Mangione, the man accused of killing the CEO of UnitedHealthcare, was charged in New York with murder as an act of terrorism, prosecutors announced Tuesday. Mangione, 26, is being held without bail in Pennsylvania, where he was arrested at a McDonald’s on Dec.
item.dc:creator: MARYCLAIRE DALE and SARAH BRUMFIELD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump, Softbank CEO announce $100B SoftBank investment in the US
item.description: resident-elect Donald Trump will join SoftBank Group CEO Masayoshi Son at his Florida home on Monday to announce that the Japanese company is planning to invest $100 billion in U.S. projects over the next four years.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Long, WGN 9
